exit_criteria: TASK-100 is executable and not an umbrella/container task.
Acceptance criteria map to all GC requirements.
Candidate files and tests are listed for implementation handoff.

loop_start.reason: Compiler loops start from source refs and build handoffs, not prior loop chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Planning validation can use deterministic task/alignment audits over roadmap/build refs.

next_loop.reason: The next compiler loop should start from the build handoff, not the producing compiler context.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

requirements.text: Tracked `.codewiki` artifacts must not be purged until an archive commit containing revive context exists.
GC must write restore evidence that names archive commit/tree, removed paths, and restore commands before deleting tracked artifacts from the hot tree.
Agent skills must make post-commit GC review mandatory at close/publication boundaries and require block/defer evidence when skipped.
A public source-owned GC path must support dry-run, scoped purge, tracked artifact gates, and ignored runtime cleanup without manual file deletion.

evidence_mapping.criterion: Archive proof gate maps to TASK-100 acceptance
evidence_mapping.evidence: TASK-100 acceptance requires tracked purge to refuse without archive commit SHA/tree proof and to avoid generated graph as source of purge truth.

evidence_mapping.criterion: Restore ledger maps to TASK-100 acceptance
evidence_mapping.evidence: TASK-100 acceptance requires ledger with removed paths and git restore commands before tracked deletion.

evidence_mapping.criterion: Skill enforcement maps to TASK-100 acceptance
evidence_mapping.evidence: TASK-100 acceptance requires main/implementation/validation skill text and prompt tests for post-commit GC review or block/defer evidence.

evidence_mapping.criterion: Public safe tool maps to TASK-100 acceptance
evidence_mapping.evidence: TASK-100 acceptance requires typed Pi adapter tool and dry-run/runtime cleanup behavior.

summary: Plan executable GC capability and skill enforcement task
task_changes: Created TASK-100 as one self-contained executable task for safe post-commit GC capability plus skill enforcement. The task was automatically refined to merge the API and skill enforcement scopes because labels/spec paths overlapped; acceptance criteria now cover both source-owned GC behavior and prompt/skill invariants.

The task was automatically refined to merge the API and skill enforcement scopes because labels/spec paths overlapped; acceptance criteria now cover both source-owned GC behavior and prompt/skill invariants.
tdd_plan: Add or extend smoke tests for GC dry-run/purge semantics before implementing the tool.
Add prompt asset assertions that relevant skills mention archive commit -> GC commit and post-commit GC review/defer evidence.
Verify package smoke registration sees the new Pi tool and schema.
Run typecheck to prove new domain/tool schema contracts compile.

assumptions: One combined task is acceptable because CodeWiki task creation merged the overlapping API and skill scopes into a single direct outcome.
Implementation can begin with dry-run and ledger generation before destructive purge behavior.
non_goals: Do not push/publish or remove remote history.
Do not perform manual tracked `.codewiki` deletion outside the new safe GC path.
Do not weaken validation or task-close content-proof gates.
risks: Task touches API, skills, and tests; keep implementation surgical and stop if scope grows beyond direct GC enforcement.
Actual tracked purge should be exercised on test fixtures before dogfood cleanup.
